---
title: "Monodomain Heart Example"
format: html
---

## Heart Simulation Example

Comprehensive examples of 4Dpapers figure embedding with cardiac electrophysiology data from the Niederer benchmark suite. See [AGENTS.md](../../AGENTS.md) for supported formats and data import guidance.

### Single Figure: Niederer Verification Example

The Niederer et al. (2011) verification benchmark is a simple slab geometry. This example shows transmembrane potential (Vm) at the final timestep with field switching enabled:

{{< 4d-image id="niederer-sim" src="/Users/simaocastro/noFrontendCardiacFoam_minor_errors/tutorials/NiedererEtAl2011/NiedererEtAl2011verification/Niederer.foam" field="Vm" fields="Vm,activationTime" time="last" caption="Niederer Verification: Transmembrane Potential (Vm) at final timestep. Live field switcher available for Vm and activationTime." >}}

### Synchronized Panel: Field Comparison

Side-by-side synchronized camera view comparing transmembrane potential (Vm) and activation time. Rotating the camera in one figure automatically rotates the other:

{{< 4d-panel id="niederer-panel" layout="2x1" height="600px" camera="sync" caption="Niederer slab: Side-by-side Vm and activationTime with synchronized cameras"
    src1="/Users/simaocastro/noFrontendCardiacFoam_minor_errors/tutorials/NiedererEtAl2011/NiedererEtAl2011verification/Niederer.foam"
    id1="niederer-vm"
    field1="Vm"
    src2="/Users/simaocastro/noFrontendCardiacFoam_minor_errors/tutorials/NiedererEtAl2011/NiedererEtAl2011verification/Niederer.foam"
    id2="niederer-act"
    field2="activationTime" >}}

### Time Series: Evolution Over Timesteps

Synchronized multi-panel view showing how transmembrane potential evolves across 4 evenly-spaced timesteps. Camera rotation in any panel updates all:

{{< 4d-timeseries id="niederer-ts" src="/Users/simaocastro/noFrontendCardiacFoam_minor_errors/tutorials/NiedererEtAl2011/NiedererEtAl2011verification/Niederer.foam" field="Vm" fields="Vm,activationTime" steps="4" height="400px" caption="Niederer verification: Transmembrane potential evolution over 4 representative timesteps." >}}


## Cardiac Electrophysiology: Heart and Purkinje Network

The figures below show the bidomain cardiac simulation from the 3D-1D coupled model. The Purkinje network (a 1D conduction system embedded in the heart wall) activates first and drives the myocardial wavefront.

### Heart alone — transmembrane potential Vm

Full 3D myocardial mesh coloured by transmembrane potential Vm (coolwarm). At t ≈ 25 ms the activation wavefront driven by the Purkinje network is clearly visible:

{{< 4d-image id="heart-vm" src="/Users/simaocastro/noFrontendCardiacFoam_minor_errors/tutorials/heartSim3D-1D/monodomainHeart/monodomainHeart.foam" field="Vm" cmap="coolwarm" decimate="auto" stride="8" time="5" caption="Myocardial transmembrane potential Vm at t ≈ 25 ms. Press ▶ to animate the full activation sequence." >}}

### Purkinje network alone — transmembrane potential Vm\_V

The 1D Purkinje conduction network rendered as tubes, coloured by Vm\_V. The network depolarises ahead of the myocardium and has fully repolarised by t ≈ 0.4 s:

{{< 4d-image id="purkinje-vm" src="/Users/simaocastro/noFrontendCardiacFoam_minor_errors/tutorials/heartSim3D-1D/monodomainHeart/postProcessing/purkinjeNetworkVTK/purkinjeNetwork.vtk.series" field="Vm_V" cmap="plasma" decimate="none" stride="8" time="5" caption="Purkinje network transmembrane potential Vm\_V at t ≈ 25 ms. Press ▶ to animate." >}}

### Combined view — myocardium + Purkinje network

Both datasets rendered as separate actors in a single vtk.js scene. The heart is 60 % opaque to reveal the Purkinje network beneath the epicardial surface. Pressing ▶ animates both simultaneously, with the Purkinje timestep snapped to the nearest physical time of the primary (heart) axis:

{{< 4d-multi-image id="heart-purkinje-combined"
    src1="/Users/simaocastro/noFrontendCardiacFoam_minor_errors/tutorials/heartSim3D-1D/monodomainHeart/monodomainHeart.foam"
    field1="Vm" cmap1="coolwarm" decimate1="auto" opacity1="0.6" colorbar1="true"
    src2="/Users/simaocastro/noFrontendCardiacFoam_minor_errors/tutorials/heartSim3D-1D/monodomainHeart/postProcessing/purkinjeNetworkVTK/purkinjeNetwork.vtk.series"
    field2="Vm_V" cmap2="plasma" decimate2="none" colorbar2="false"
    time="5" stride="8"
    caption="Coupled 3D-1D cardiac simulation: myocardium (coolwarm) and Purkinje network (plasma) at t ≈ 25 ms. Both datasets animate synchronously." >}}

## Static Images & Diagrams

4Dpapers papers frequently include static images alongside interactive 3D figures—methodology diagrams, photos, plots, schematics, or reference images. Use Quarto's native Markdown syntax for these:

### Basic Image

```markdown
![Heart diagram](data/heart-diagram.png)
```

### With Figure Width & Caption

```markdown
![Cardiac geometry sketch showing myocardium (red) and Purkinje network (blue) pathways.](data/cardiac-anatomy.png){width=60% fig-align="center"}
```

### Multiple Images in a Row

```markdown
![Left: Transmembrane potential scale.](data/vm-scale.png){width=48%}
![Right: Activation time scale.](data/activation-scale.png){width=48%}
```

### Numbered Figures with Reference

```markdown
![My reference image](data/setup.png){#fig-setup}

See @fig-setup for the experimental setup.
```

**Tip:** Place static images in the `data/` directory alongside your 3D case files. Quarto will automatically embed them in both HTML (web) and PDF (static) outputs. No shortcode parsing needed—just plain Markdown.
